zh.Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Hill於1517年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲時，他的父親James Hill24歲，母親Margaret Ware22歲。他於1540年與Joan Annable在英國英格蘭素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1582年10月18日之後在英國素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Hill于1517年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲时，他的父亲James Hill24岁，母亲Margaret Ware22岁。他于1540年与Joan Annable在英国英格兰素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1582年10月18日之后在英国素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲。

nameMeanings.name: Hill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

variants.name: Hillock
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) small hill’, from Middle English hillok, a diminutive of hille (Old English hyll ‘hill’). See Hill .

variants.name: Hills
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hiller
meanings: German: metronymic from Hille, a pet form of the female personal name Hildegund (see Hilke ).
German: from a masculine personal beginning with the ancient Germanic element hild ‘strife, battle’.

variants.name: Hil
meanings: Dutch (Van den Hil): topographic name from hil, hul ‘hill’, particularly in the sense of a flight hill above sea level in the southwestern Netherlands.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When William Hill was born in 1517, in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, James Hill, was 24 and his mother, Margaret Ware, was 22. He married Joan Annable in 1540, in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He died after 18 October 1582, in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England.

Il a épousé Joan Annable en 1540, à Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé après 18 octobre 1582, à Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Angleterre.

ko.Bury Saint Edmunds, West Suffolk distric: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈
ko.lifeSummary: William Hill(이)가 1517년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Hill(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Ware(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1540년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈에서 Joan Annable(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1582년 10월 18일 이후 영국 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈에서 사망했다.
